metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_4584
metadata.comment:
Rationale: - K+-channel tetramerization domain-containing proteins (KCTDs) are auxiliary GABAB receptor (GBR) subunits.
- Experiments were performed at the synaptic connection from medial habenula (MHb) to interpeduncular nucleus (IPN).
- pre-embedding immunogold labeling, imaged with TEM. "In MHb terminals in the rostral IPN, Cav2.3 and KCTD12b showed similar localization patterns, with peak particle densities in the active zone and a gradual decrease in density with increased distance from the active zone (Figure 3A,E)."

- Figure 2C: immunolabeling of WT and KCTD12b KO mice shows antibody specificity. Experimental description: Wild-type C57BL/6J mice(Jax, Bar Harbor, ME; #000664) purchased from Jackson Laboratory.
Potassium channel tetramerisation domain-containing 12b
Antibody: anti-KCTD12b (polyclonal, raised against a synthetic peptide comprised of the N-terminal amino acids 1-16 of KCTD12b) (1 ug/ul, 1 O/N, Bettler lab, Univ. Basel)

checks.label: OWL Consistency
checks.error_message: No inferences attributed to this model graph in the materializer output. Likely a parse failure or other materializer skip — re-run on this model alone to investigate.
